title: Proportional pinch valve
abstract: A proportional pinch valve (10) for controlling the pressure of a fluid in a continuous flow system comprises an anvil (22) for pinching a length of a tubing (12) in the continuous flow system, and a drive mechanism including a displacement element for moving the anvil towards the tubing (12). The anvil (22) is indirectly coupled to the displacement element via an elastic spring element (36). The elastic spring element (36) provides a defined play (i.e. elasticity) for the anvil (22) at least as long as the tubing (12) is not fully pinched so that displacement of the anvil (22) is force-controlled by the elastic spring element (36). A method of controlling the pressure of a fluid in a continuous flow system makes use of such a proportional pinch valve (10).
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A proportional pinch valve (10) for controlling the pressure in a continuous flow system, the proportional pinch valve (10) comprising an anvil (22) for pinching a length of a tubing (12) in the continuous flow system, and a drive mechanism including a displacement element for moving the anvil towards the tubing (12), the anvil (22) being indirectly coupled to the displacement element via an elastic spring element (36), the elastic spring element (36) providing a defined play for the anvil (22) at least as long as the tubing (12) is not fully pinched so that displacement of the anvil (22) is force-controlled by the elastic spring element (36). 2. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to claim 1 , characterised in that the spring element (36) is a coil spring. 3. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to claim 1 or claim 2, characterised in that the displacement element is a leadscrew nut (32) arranged on a leadscrew (34). 4. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to claim 2 and claim 3, characterised in that one end of the coil spring is supported on the leadscrew nut (32) and the other end of the coil spring is supported on the anvil (22). 5. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims, characterised in that the drive mechanism includes an automated drive, preferably a stepper motor (40), for displacing the displacement element. 6. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims, characterised by a support member (16) having one or more receptacles (14) and a cover plate (18) cooperating with the support member (16) for holding the tubing (12). 7. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims, characterised in that the length of a front surface of the anvil (22) contacting the 

 tubing (12) to the diameter of the non-pinched tubing (12) is greater than 5 mm, preferably in the range of 5 to 10 mm. 8. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims, characterised by a ridge (50) projecting from a front face of the anvil (22) facing the tubing (12). 9. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to claim 8, characterised in that the ridge (50) extends perpendicular to a longitudinal direction of the tubing (12). 10. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims, characterised by a seal, preferably a rubber diaphragm (26), for sealing a free space (28) around the pinched tubing (12) from the drive mechanism. 1 1 . The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims, characterised by a pin (44) coupled to the displacement element, the pin (44) cooperating with a slot (46) decoupled from the displacement element in order to prevent the displacement element from unintentional rotation. 12. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims, characterised by a position sensor (48) for detecting at least a home position of the displacement element. 13. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims, characterised in that the displacement element can be displaced into an end position in which the tubing (12) is fully pinched, the displacement element being in contact with the anvil (22) in the end position. 14. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of claims 1 to 12, characterised in that the displacement element can be displaced into an end position in which the tubing (12) is fully pinched, the displacement element not being in contact with the anvil (22) in the end position. 15. The proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims, characterised by a switch mechanism for switching the driving mechanism between a first mode, in which the anvil (22) is directly coupled to the displacement element, and a second mode, in which the anvil (22) is indirectly coupled to the displacement element via the spring element (36). 16. A method of controlling the pressure of a fluid in a continuous flow system using a proportional pinch valve (10) according to any of the preceding claims. 17. The method of claim 16, characterised in that the elastic spring element (36) provides a defined play for the anvil (22) at least as long as the tubing (12) is not fully pinched so that displacement of the anvil (22) is force-controlled by the elastic spring element (36). 18. The method of claim 17, characterised in that in response to a backpressure acting on the fluid flowing through the tubing (12) the pinched surfaces of the tubing (12) are forced apart against the bias of the elastic spring element (36). 19. The method of any of claims 16 to 18, characterised in that the continuous flow system is a cross-flow filtration system.
